children.name: Aix
children.description: A genus of 2 ornate perching ducks — the Mandarin Duck of East Asia and the Wood Duck of North America — noted for their spectacular iridescent plumage and habit of nesting in tree cavities.

children.name: Aix galericulata
children.description: The mandarin duck is a perching duck species native to East and Northeast Asia. It is sexually dimorphic; the males are elaborately coloured, while the females have more subdued colours. It is a medium-sized duck, at 41–49 cm (16–19 in) long with a 65–75 cm (26–30 in) wingspan.

children.name: Aix sponsa
children.description: Aix sponsa (Wood Duck) is a perching duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Alopochen
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Egyptian Goose, a large, boldly patterned sheldgoose native to Africa south of the Sahara and the Nile Valley, now widely introduced in Europe.

children.name: Alopochen aegyptiaca
children.description: Alopochen aegyptiaca (Egyptian Goose) is a goose species found in Africa region.

children.name: Amazonetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Brazilian Teal, a small, chestnut-and-grey dabbling duck endemic to freshwater wetlands and gallery forests of South America.

children.name: Amazonetta brasiliensis
children.description: Amazonetta brasiliensis (Brazilian Teal) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Anas
children.description: The largest genus of dabbling ducks with about 30 species, including the cosmopolitan Mallard, distributed across every continent; members feed by tipping head-down in shallow water rather than diving.

children.name: Anas acuta
children.description: Anas acuta (Northern Pintail) is a dabbling duck species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Anas albogularis
children.description: Anas albogularis (Andaman Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Anas andium
children.description: Anas andium (Andean Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Anas aucklandica
children.description: Anas aucklandica (Auckland Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Anas bahamensis
children.description: Anas bahamensis (White-cheeked Pintail) is a dabbling duck species found in South America and North America regions.

children.name: Anas bernieri
children.description: Anas bernieri (Bernier's Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Anas capensis
children.description: Anas capensis (Cape Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Anas carolinensis
children.description: Anas carolinensis (Green-winged Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Anas castanea
children.description: Anas castanea (Chestnut Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Australia region.

children.name: Anas chlorotis
children.description: Anas chlorotis (Brown Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Anas crecca
children.description: The Eurasian teal, common teal, or Eurasian green-winged teal is a common and widespread duck that breeds in temperate Eurosiberia and migrates south in winter. The Eurasian teal is often called simply the teal due to being the only one of these small dabbling ducks in much of its range. The bird gives its name to the blue-green colour teal.

children.name: Anas diazi
children.description: Anas diazi (Mexican Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Anas eatoni
children.description: A small flightless teal restricted to the Kerguelen Islands in the southern Indian Ocean, one of the most isolated land masses on Earth. Monotypic; formerly lumped with the Crozet Teal as a single species but the two island populations have diverged both genetically and morphologically. Feeds on invertebrates, algae, and carrion along stream margins and coastal pools. Its wings are reduced and it cannot fly, an adaptation common to island teals that evolved in the absence of land predators — though introduced cats and rats now threaten it. IUCN Vulnerable. Named after Alfred Eaton, the British naturalist who collected specimens during the 1874–75 Transit of Venus expedition.

children.name: Anas drygalskii
children.description: A small, near-flightless teal endemic to the Crozet Islands in the southern Indian Ocean, split from the Kerguelen Teal on the basis of morphological and genetic differences. Like its Kerguelen relative it has reduced wing musculature and very limited flight capability — a consequence of evolving on islands without mammalian predators. Feeds on aquatic invertebrates in sheltered bogs, streams and coastal pools on the remote subantarctic archipelago. Named after Erich von Drygalski, the German geographer and polar explorer. IUCN Near Threatened.

children.name: Anas erythrorhyncha
children.description: Anas erythrorhyncha (Red-billed Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Anas flavirostris
children.description: Anas flavirostris (Yellow-billed Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Anas fulvigula
children.description: Anas fulvigula (Mottled Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Anas georgica
children.description: Anas georgica (Yellow-billed Pintail) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Anas gibberifrons
children.description: Anas gibberifrons (Sunda Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Anas gracilis
children.description: Anas gracilis (Grey Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Australia and Asia regions.

children.name: Anas laysanensis
children.description: Anas laysanensis (Laysan Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Anas luzonica
children.description: Anas luzonica (Philippine Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Anas melleri
children.description: Anas melleri (Meller's Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Anas nesiotis
children.description: Anas nesiotis (Campbell Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Anas platyrhynchos
children.description: The mallard or wild duck is a dabbling duck that breeds throughout the temperate and subtropical Americas, Eurasia, and North Africa. It has been introduced to New Zealand, Australia, Peru, Brazil, Uruguay, Argentina, Chile, Colombia, the Falkland Islands, and South Africa. Belonging to the subfamily Anatinae of the waterfowl family Anatidae, mallards live in wetlands, eat water plants and small animals, and are social animals preferring to congregate in groups or flocks of varying sizes.

children.name: Anas platyrhynchos domesticus
children.description: Anas platyrhynchos domesticus (Domestic Duck) is a domesticated waterfowl species found worldwide.

children.name: Anas poecilorhyncha
children.description: A large dabbling duck of the Indian subcontinent, named for the distinctive yellow-tipped bill with a bright orange-red base that provides its common name. Breeds on freshwater lakes, rivers and marshes across the Indian peninsula and Sri Lanka. A close relative of the mallard with which it will hybridise where ranges overlap. The nominate subspecies; now treated as a separate species from the Burmese Spot-billed Duck following genetic and morphological evidence of significant divergence across their range boundary in Myanmar.

children.name: Anas haringtoni
children.description: A large dabbling duck of mainland Southeast Asia, recently elevated from a subspecies of the Indian Spot-billed Duck on the basis of genetic and morphological differences. Its range extends from Myanmar and southern China through Indochina to the Malay Peninsula. Distinguished from the Indian nominate by subtler plumage differences, particularly in the facial pattern and speculum markings. Found on rivers, lakes, and paddy fields throughout the lowlands of mainland Southeast Asia. Named after Harington, the British officer who collected specimens in Burma in the early 20th century.

children.name: Anas rubripes
children.description: Anas rubripes (American Black Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Anas sparsa
children.description: A medium-sized dark duck of fast-flowing rivers in southern Africa, from the Cape to Zimbabwe and Mozambique. Distinguished from the East African Black Duck by its more southerly range and by subtle but consistent plumage differences in the wing pattern and scapulars. Typically found in pairs on clear, rocky mountain streams and rivers where it forages for invertebrates and aquatic plants. One of relatively few African ducks to favour running water over still wetlands; its powerful bill and strong claws are well adapted to feeding in swift currents.

children.name: Anas leucostigma
children.description: A dark, river-dwelling duck of the East African highlands and Rift Valley, elevated from a subspecies of the African Black Duck on the basis of its disjunct range and morphological distinctiveness. Found from Ethiopia and Sudan south through Kenya and Uganda to Tanzania, inhabiting fast-flowing rivers and streams in montane and highland zones. Like its southern relative it favours clear, rocky watercourses over still water. The name leucostigma ('white-marked') refers to the white speckling on the dark plumage. Its isolation in East African highland river systems has led to consistent differences in plumage and size that support species-level recognition.

children.name: Anas superciliosa
children.description: Anas superciliosa (Pacific Black Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Australia and Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Anas undulata
children.description: Anas undulata (Yellow-billed Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Anas wyvilliana
children.description: Anas wyvilliana (Hawaiian Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Anas zonorhyncha
children.description: Anas zonorhyncha (Eastern Spot-billed Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Anas oustaleti
children.description: An extinct mallard-type duck endemic to the Mariana Islands of the western Pacific, believed to have arisen from hybridisation between the Mallard and the Pacific Black Duck. It inhabited lowland wetlands and rice paddies and was last recorded in 1979 on Saipan; it is now considered extinct, driven to extinction by overhunting and wholesale habitat loss from military and agricultural development.

children.name: Anser
children.description: A genus of 11 grey geese — including the Greylag, Bean, Snow, and Bar-headed Goose — breeding across the Holarctic and notable for long-distance migrations between Arctic breeding grounds and temperate wintering areas.

children.name: Anser albifrons
children.description: Anser albifrons (Greater White-fronted Goose) is a goose species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Anser anser
children.description: Anser anser (Greylag Goose) is a goose species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Anser anser domesticus
children.description: Anser anser domesticus (Domestic Goose) is a domesticated waterfowl species found worldwide.

children.name: Anser brachyrhynchus
children.description: Anser brachyrhynchus (Pink-footed Goose) is a goose species found in Europe region.

children.name: Anser caerulescens
children.description: Anser caerulescens (Snow Goose) is a goose species found in North America region.

children.name: Anser canagicus
children.description: Anser canagicus (Emperor Goose) is a goose species found in North America and Asia regions.

children.name: Anser cygnoides
children.description: Anser cygnoides (Swan Goose) is a goose species found in Asia region.

children.name: Anser erythropus
children.description: Anser erythropus (Lesser White-fronted Goose) is a goose species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Anser fabalis
children.description: Anser fabalis (Bean Goose) is a goose species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Anser indicus
children.description: Anser indicus (Bar-headed Goose) is a goose species found in Asia region.

children.name: Anser rossii
children.description: Anser rossii (Ross's Goose) is a goose species found in North America region.

children.name: Anser serrirostris
children.description: Anser serrirostris (Tundra Bean Goose) is a goose species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Asarcornis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the endangered White-winged Duck, a large, dark perching duck with white wing patches restricted to lowland forest rivers and wetlands of South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Asarcornis scutulata
children.description: Asarcornis scutulata (White-winged Duck) is a perching duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Aythya
children.description: A genus of 12 diving ducks — including pochards, scaups, and the Canvasback — found across the Northern Hemisphere, characterised by their compact bodies and ability to dive deeply for aquatic plants and invertebrates.

children.name: Aythya affinis
children.description: Aythya affinis (Lesser Scaup) is a diving duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Aythya americana
children.description: Aythya americana (Redhead) is a diving duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Aythya australis
children.description: Aythya australis (Hardhead) is a diving duck species found in Australia and Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Aythya baeri
children.description: Aythya baeri (Baer's Pochard) is a diving duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Aythya collaris
children.description: Aythya collaris (Ring-necked Duck) is a diving duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Aythya ferina
children.description: Aythya ferina (Common Pochard) is a diving duck species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Aythya fuligula
children.description: Aythya fuligula (Tufted Duck) is a diving duck species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Aythya innotata
children.description: Aythya innotata (Madagascar Pochard) is a diving duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Aythya marila
children.description: Aythya marila (Greater Scaup) is a diving duck species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Aythya novaeseelandiae
children.description: Aythya novaeseelandiae (New Zealand Scaup) is a diving duck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Aythya nyroca
children.description: Aythya nyroca (Ferruginous Duck) is a diving duck species found in Europe and Asia and Africa regions.

children.name: Aythya valisineria
children.description: Aythya valisineria (Canvasback) is a diving duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Biziura
children.description: A monotypic Australian genus containing the Musk Duck, a large stiff-tailed diving duck remarkable for the leathery lobe hanging beneath the male's bill and its distinctive musky odour.

children.name: Biziura lobata
children.description: Biziura lobata (Musk Duck) is a stifftail species found in Australia region.

children.name: Branta
children.description: A genus of 6 black geese — including the Canada Goose, Barnacle Goose, and Brent Goose — distinguished from grey Anser geese by their dark plumage with contrasting white face or neck patches, breeding in Arctic and subarctic regions worldwide.

children.name: Branta bernicla
children.description: Branta bernicla (Brent Goose) is a goose species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Branta canadensis
children.description: Branta canadensis (Canada Goose) is a goose species found in North America and Europe regions.

children.name: Branta hutchinsii
children.description: Branta hutchinsii (Cackling Goose) is a goose species found in North America region.

children.name: Branta leucopsis
children.description: Branta leucopsis (Barnacle Goose) is a goose species found in Europe region.

children.name: Branta ruficollis
children.description: Branta ruficollis (Red-breasted Goose) is a goose species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Branta sandvicensis
children.description: Branta sandvicensis (Nēnē) is a goose species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Bucephala
children.description: A genus of 3 goldeneye and bufflehead diving ducks of the Northern Hemisphere, characterised by large rounded heads, rapid wingbeats producing a whistling sound in flight, and nest cavities in trees near boreal lakes.

children.name: Bucephala albeola
children.description: Bucephala albeola (Bufflehead) is a bucephala species found in North America region.

children.name: Bucephala clangula
children.description: Bucephala clangula (Common Goldeneye) is a bucephala species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Bucephala islandica
children.description: Bucephala islandica (Barrow's Goldeneye) is a bucephala species found in North America region.

children.name: Cairina
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Muscovy Duck, a large, iridescent black-and-white perching duck native to Central and South America that is widely domesticated worldwide for meat production.

children.name: Cairina moschata
children.description: Cairina moschata (Muscovy Duck) is a perching duck species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Camptorhynchus
children.description: An extinct monotypic genus containing the Labrador Duck, a sea duck that inhabited the Atlantic coast of North America and became the first North American bird species confirmed extinct in the modern era.

children.name: Camptorhynchus labradorius
children.description: The Labrador Duck was a medium-sized sea duck of the Atlantic coast of North America, breeding in Labrador and wintering on sandy bays and estuaries from Nova Scotia to Chesapeake Bay, where it used its broad, flattened bill to filter molluscs and crustaceans from the shallows. It was never common and its decline was swift; the last confirmed individual was shot in 1875 off Long Island, New York. The causes of its extinction remain incompletely understood but likely include hunting and possibly low population densities even before European contact.

children.name: Callonetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Ringed Teal, a small, colourful dabbling duck of subtropical South American forests and wetlands, unusual among ducks in that the male assists with chick-rearing.

children.name: Callonetta leucophrys
children.description: Callonetta leucophrys (Ringed Teal) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Cereopsis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Cape Barren Goose, a large, pale grey goose with a distinctive yellow-green cere found on islands off southern Australia, unusual for a waterfowl in its preference for grazing on dry land.

children.name: Cereopsis novaehollandiae
children.description: Cereopsis novaehollandiae (Cape Barren Goose) is a cereopsis species found in Australia region.

children.name: Chenonetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Australian Wood Duck, a goose-like dabbling duck widespread across grasslands and lightly wooded areas of Australia, often seen grazing far from water.

children.name: Chenonetta jubata
children.description: Chenonetta jubata (Australian Wood Duck) is a chenonetta species found in Australia region.

children.name: Chloephaga
children.description: A genus of 5 South American sheldgeese — including the Kelp Goose and Upland Goose — mostly found in Patagonia and the Falkland Islands, where they graze on grasslands and coastal meadows.

children.name: Chloephaga hybrida
children.description: Chloephaga hybrida (Kelp Goose) is a goose species found in South America region.

children.name: Chloephaga melanoptera
children.description: Chloephaga melanoptera (Andean Goose) is a goose species found in South America region.

children.name: Chloephaga picta
children.description: Chloephaga picta (Upland Goose) is a goose species found in South America region.

children.name: Chloephaga poliocephala
children.description: Chloephaga poliocephala (Ashy-headed Goose) is a goose species found in South America region.

children.name: Chloephaga rubidiceps
children.description: Chloephaga rubidiceps (Ruddy-headed Goose) is a goose species found in South America region.

children.name: Clangula
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Long-tailed Duck, an Arctic sea duck recognisable by the male's long tail streamers and complex seasonal plumage, which dives to extraordinary depths of up to 60 m to feed.

children.name: Clangula hyemalis
children.description: Clangula hyemalis (Long-tailed Duck) is a clangula species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Coscoroba
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Coscoroba Swan, the smallest swan, entirely white with red bill and feet, found in southern South America from central Chile and Argentina to Tierra del Fuego.

children.name: Coscoroba coscoroba
children.description: Coscoroba coscoroba (Coscoroba Swan) is a swan species found in South America region.

children.name: Cyanochen
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Blue-winged Goose, a small, brown Afrotropical sheldgoose endemic to the Ethiopian Highlands, where it grazes on montane grasslands at high altitudes.

children.name: Cyanochen cyanoptera
children.description: Cyanochen cyanoptera (Blue-winged Goose) is a cyanochen species found in Africa region.

children.name: Cygnus
children.description: A genus of 6 swans — the largest waterfowl — including the Mute, Whooper, Trumpeter, and Black Swan, found on every continent except Antarctica, characterised by long necks, powerful wingbeats, and lifelong pair bonds.

children.name: Cygnus atratus
children.description: Cygnus atratus (Black Swan) is a swan species found in Australia region.

children.name: Cygnus buccinator
children.description: Cygnus buccinator (Trumpeter Swan) is a swan species found in North America region.

children.name: Cygnus columbianus
children.description: The North American tundra swan, breeding on Arctic tundra from Alaska to the Hudson Bay coast and wintering on the Pacific and Atlantic coasts of the United States. Formerly lumped with Bewick's Swan as a single species (the Tundra Swan) but now widely treated as separate. Distinguished from its Palearctic counterpart by its all-black bill (Bewick's has a yellow base), slightly larger size, and different voice. Famously described by the explorer Meriwether Lewis during the Corps of Discovery expedition — its musical, resonant calls have been described as whistling, giving the species its common name.

children.name: Cygnus bewickii
children.description: The Palearctic tundra swan, breeding on Arctic tundra from the Yamal Peninsula east across Siberia to Chukotka, wintering in western Europe (primarily the Netherlands and eastern England) and in eastern Asia (Japan, Korea, China). Distinguished from the Whistling Swan by the distinctive yellow patch at the base of the black bill — uniquely patterned in each individual, allowing long-term recognition of specific swans in wintering flocks. The pioneering studies by Sir Peter Scott at the Wildfowl Trust at Slimbridge identified hundreds of individually recognisable birds over decades. Named after the engraver Thomas Bewick, celebrated for his wood-engravings of birds and quadrupeds.

children.name: Cygnus cygnus
children.description: The whooper swan, also known as the common swan, is a large northern hemisphere swan. It is the Eurasian counterpart of the North American trumpeter swan.

children.name: Cygnus melancoryphus
children.description: Cygnus melancoryphus (Black-necked Swan) is a swan species found in South America region.

children.name: Cygnus olor
children.description: The mute swan is a species of swan and a member of the family Anatidae within the waterfowl order. It is native to much of Europe and parts of Asia and the far north of Africa. It is an introduced species in North America, home to the largest populations outside of its native range, with additional smaller introductions in Australasia and Southern Africa.

children.name: Dendrocygna
children.description: A genus of 8 whistling-ducks with a pantropical distribution across the Americas, Africa, and Asia-Pacific, distinguished by their upright stance, long necks and legs, and loud whistling calls given in flight.

children.name: Dendrocygna arborea
children.description: Dendrocygna arborea (West Indian Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in North America region.

children.name: Dendrocygna arcuata
children.description: Dendrocygna arcuata (Wandering Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in Asia and Australia regions.

children.name: Dendrocygna autumnalis
children.description: Dendrocygna autumnalis (Black-bellied Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Dendrocygna bicolor
children.description: Dendrocygna bicolor (Fulvous Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in North America and South America and Africa and Asia regions.

children.name: Dendrocygna eytoni
children.description: Dendrocygna eytoni (Plumed Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in Australia region.

children.name: Dendrocygna guttata
children.description: Dendrocygna guttata (Spotted Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in Asia region.

children.name: Dendrocygna javanica
children.description: Dendrocygna javanica (Lesser Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in Asia region.

children.name: Dendrocygna viduata
children.description: Dendrocygna viduata (White-faced Whistling Duck) is a dendrocygna species found in Africa and South America regions.

children.name: Heteronetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Black-headed Duck of southern South America, the only obligate brood parasite in the duck family, laying its eggs exclusively in the nests of other waterbirds.

children.name: Heteronetta atricapilla
children.description: Heteronetta atricapilla (Black-headed Duck) is a stifftail species found in South America region.

children.name: Histrionicus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Harlequin Duck, a small sea duck with boldly patterned blue, chestnut, and white plumage that breeds along fast-flowing mountain torrents in North America and Iceland, wintering on rocky coastlines.

children.name: Histrionicus histrionicus
children.description: Histrionicus histrionicus (Harlequin Duck) is a histrionicus species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Hymenolaimus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Blue Duck, a torrent-specialist endemic to fast-flowing mountain streams of New Zealand, with streamlined body, pink fleshy bill lobes, and chestnut-spotted blue-grey plumage.

children.name: Hymenolaimus malacorhynchos
children.description: Hymenolaimus malacorhynchos (Blue Duck) is a hymenolaimus species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Lophodytes
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Hooded Merganser, a small North American fish-eating duck renowned for the male's large fan-shaped white crest edged in black, which nests in tree cavities near wooded lakes and ponds.

children.name: Lophodytes cucullatus
children.description: Lophodytes cucullatus (Hooded Merganser) is a merganser species found in North America region.

children.name: Lophonetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Duck, a large, pale brownish dabbling duck with a small wispy crest found along coasts, rivers, and high-altitude lakes of Patagonia and the Andes.

children.name: Lophonetta specularioides
children.description: Lophonetta specularioides (Crested Duck) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Malacorhynchus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Pink-eared Duck, a small Australian dabbling duck with a unique large spatula-shaped bill fringed with lamellae for filter-feeding, and a distinctive black-and-white barred pattern.

children.name: Malacorhynchus membranaceus
children.description: Malacorhynchus membranaceus (Pink-eared Duck) is a malacorhynchus species found in Australia region.

children.name: Mareca
children.description: A genus of 6 dabbling ducks (including 1 extinct species) known as wigeons and the Falcated Duck, found across the Holarctic and South America, characterised by rounded heads, short bills, and a preference for grazing on short grass and aquatic vegetation.

children.name: Mareca americana
children.description: The American wigeon, also known as the baldpate, is a species of dabbling duck found in North America. Formerly assigned to Anas, this species is classified with the other wigeons in the dabbling duck genus Mareca. It is the New World counterpart of the Eurasian wigeon.

children.name: Mareca falcata
children.description: Mareca falcata (Falcated Duck) is a dabbling duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Mareca penelope
children.description: The Eurasian wigeon or European wigeon, also known as the widgeon or wigeon, is one of three extant species of wigeon in the dabbling duck genus Mareca. It is common and widespread within its Palearctic range.

children.name: Mareca sibilatrix
children.description: Mareca sibilatrix (Chiloe Wigeon) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Mareca strepera
children.description: Mareca strepera (Gadwall) is a dabbling duck species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Mareca couesi
children.description: An extinct island gadwall endemic to Fanning Atoll (now Tabuaeran) in the central Pacific Ocean, known from a small number of specimens collected by the USS Tanager expedition in the 1870s. Closely related to the Gadwall, it represented a distinct island form that succumbed to hunting pressure and predation by introduced animals. Named after American army surgeon and naturalist Elliott Coues.

children.name: Marmaronetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Marbled Duck, a pale mottled diving-dabbling duck of shallow, vegetated wetlands from the Mediterranean to Central Asia, considered vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Marmaronetta angustirostris
children.description: Marmaronetta angustirostris (Marbled Duck) is a marmaronetta species found in Europe and Asia and Africa regions.

children.name: Melanitta
children.description: A genus of 6 scoters — large, mostly black sea ducks of the Northern Hemisphere — that breed on Arctic and boreal lakes but winter in large flocks on coastal seas, diving for molluscs and crustaceans.

children.name: Melanitta americana
children.description: Melanitta americana (Black Scoter) is a melanitta species found in North America region.

children.name: Melanitta deglandi
children.description: Melanitta deglandi (White-winged Scoter) is a melanitta species found in North America region.

children.name: Melanitta fusca
children.description: Melanitta fusca (Velvet Scoter) is a melanitta species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Melanitta nigra
children.description: Melanitta nigra (Common Scoter) is a melanitta species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Melanitta perspicillata
children.description: Melanitta perspicillata (Surf Scoter) is a melanitta species found in North America region.

children.name: Melanitta stejnegeri
children.description: Melanitta stejnegeri (Siberian Scoter) is a melanitta species found in Asia region.

children.name: Merganetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Torrent Duck, a striking Andean specialist that forages in fast-flowing Andean rivers and streams from Venezuela to Tierra del Fuego, with a streamlined body and stiff tail for navigating white water.

children.name: Merganetta armata
children.description: Merganetta armata (Torrent Duck) is a merganetta species found in South America region.

children.name: Mergellus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Smew, a small Eurasian merganser in which the white-and-black male is among the most elegant of ducks, breeding in boreal forest lakes and wintering on European and Asian coasts.

children.name: Mergellus albellus
children.description: Mergellus albellus (Smew) is a merganser species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Mergus
children.description: A genus of 5 mergansers (including 1 extinct species) — including the Common Merganser and Red-breasted Merganser — found across the Holarctic, characterised by serrated bills for gripping fish and a long, streamlined body for underwater pursuit.

children.name: Mergus merganser
children.description: Mergus merganser (Common Merganser) is a merganser species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Mergus octosetaceus
children.description: Mergus octosetaceus (Brazilian Merganser) is a merganser species found in South America region.

children.name: Mergus serrator
children.description: Mergus serrator (Red-breasted Merganser) is a merganser species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Mergus squamatus
children.description: Mergus squamatus (Scaly-sided Merganser) is a merganser species found in Asia region.

children.name: Mergus australis
children.description: An extinct merganser that was endemic to the Auckland Islands, a remote subantarctic archipelago south of New Zealand, where it inhabited freshwater streams and coastal shores. Essentially flightless, it fed on fish, crustaceans, and invertebrates using its serrated bill. Last recorded alive in 1902, it was exterminated by hunting from visiting sealers and whalers combined with predation by introduced pigs, cats, and rats.

children.name: Neochen
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Orinoco Goose, a pale-bodied sheldgoose with chestnut flanks found along forested river banks and sandbars in the Amazon and Orinoco basins of South America.

children.name: Neochen jubata
children.description: Neochen jubata (Orinoco Goose) is a goose species found in South America region.

children.name: Netta
children.description: A genus of 4 pochards — the Red-crested Pochard, Rosy-billed Pochard, Southern Pochard, and African Pochard — medium-sized diving ducks distributed across Europe, Africa, and South America, often found on large open lakes.

children.name: Netta brunnea
children.description: A medium-sized diving pochard of sub-Saharan African freshwater lakes and reservoirs, split from the South American Southern Pochard (N. erythrophthalma) on the basis of morphological and genetic differentiation. The male has a dark reddish-brown head, white flanks and a pale blue-grey bill; the female is browner overall. Found from Ethiopia and the Congo basin south to the Cape, favouring deep, open waters with fringing vegetation. Historically common but undergoing significant declines due to wetland drainage, hunting pressure, and capture for the live bird trade. IUCN Vulnerable.

children.name: Netta erythrophthalma
children.description: A medium-sized diving duck of South American freshwater lakes, the South American counterpart to the African Pochard from which it is now treated as specifically distinct. Found from Colombia and Venezuela south through Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, and Argentina. The male has a deep chestnut-red head and neck, contrasting white flanks and a pale blue-grey bill; females are brown with a distinctive pale face pattern. Inhabits open lakes and ponds with emergent vegetation, diving for aquatic plants and invertebrates. The species name erythrophthalma means 'red-eyed'.

children.name: Netta peposaca
children.description: Netta peposaca (Rosy-billed Pochard) is a diving duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Netta rufina
children.description: Netta rufina (Red-crested Pochard) is a diving duck species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Nettapus
children.description: A genus of 3 pygmy geese — the smallest waterfowl — found on tropical wetlands across sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and Australasia, with short goose-like bills and a preference for floating vegetation.

children.name: Nettapus auritus
children.description: Nettapus auritus (African Pygmy Goose) is a perching duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Nettapus coromandelianus
children.description: Nettapus coromandelianus (Cotton Pygmy Goose) is a perching duck species found in Asia and Australia regions.

children.name: Nettapus pulchellus
children.description: Nettapus pulchellus (Green Pygmy Goose) is a perching duck species found in Australia region.

children.name: Nomonyx
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Masked Duck, a small, secretive stiff-tailed duck of tropical freshwater marshes from Mexico and the Caribbean through South America, closely related to Oxyura.

children.name: Nomonyx dominicus
children.description: Nomonyx dominicus (Masked Duck) is a stifftail species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Oxyura
children.description: A genus of 6 stiff-tailed ducks found on freshwater lakes in the Americas, Europe, Africa, and Australia, distinguished by their stiff fan-like tails held erect during courtship and compact diving bodies.

children.name: Oxyura australis
children.description: Oxyura australis (Blue-billed Duck) is a stifftail species found in Australia region.

children.name: Oxyura ferruginea
children.description: Oxyura ferruginea (Andean Duck) is a stifftail species found in South America region.

children.name: Oxyura jamaicensis
children.description: Oxyura jamaicensis (Ruddy Duck) is a stifftail species found in North America region.

children.name: Oxyura leucocephala
children.description: Oxyura leucocephala (White-headed Duck) is a stifftail species found in Europe and Asia and Africa regions.

children.name: Oxyura maccoa
children.description: Oxyura maccoa (Maccoa Duck) is a stifftail species found in Africa region.

children.name: Oxyura vittata
children.description: Oxyura vittata (Lake Duck) is a stifftail species found in South America region.

children.name: Plectropterus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Spur-winged Goose, Africa's largest waterfowl, a black-and-white sheldgoose with a prominent carpal spur on each wing, widespread in sub-Saharan African wetlands and grasslands.

children.name: Plectropterus gambensis
children.description: Plectropterus gambensis (Spur-winged Goose) is a plectropterus species found in Africa region.

children.name: Polysticta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing Steller's Eider, the smallest of the four eider species, breeding on tundra wetlands of Alaska and Arctic Russia and wintering in dense flocks on shallow Alaskan and Scandinavian coasts.

children.name: Polysticta stelleri
children.description: Polysticta stelleri (Steller's Eider) is a eider species found in North America and Asia and Europe regions.

children.name: Pteronetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing Hartlaub's Duck, a dark chestnut-and-black perching duck of West and Central African tropical forest streams and rivers, named after German ornithologist Gustav Hartlaub.

children.name: Pteronetta hartlaubii
children.description: Pteronetta hartlaubii (Hartlaub's Duck) is a perching duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Radjah
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Radjah Shelduck, a white-and-chestnut shelduck of mangroves, tidal flats, and coastal wetlands in New Guinea and northern Australia, also known as the Burdekin Duck.

children.name: Radjah radjah
children.description: Radjah radjah (Raja Shelduck) is a shelduck species found in Australia and Asia regions.

children.name: Rhodonessa
children.description: An extinct monotypic genus containing the Pink-headed Duck, a strikingly coloured diving duck of South Asian swamplands, last recorded in the wild in 1935 and now feared extinct.

children.name: Rhodonessa caryophyllacea
children.description: The Pink-headed Duck was a large, distinctive diving duck of the tall-grass swamps, marshes, and pools of the Gangetic plains in India, Nepal, and Bangladesh, unmistakable for the male's deep rose-pink head and neck contrasting with dark brown body plumage. Once locally common, it was heavily hunted for sport and its populations collapsed through the 19th and early 20th centuries; the last reliable wild sightings were from Bihar, India in 1935, though unverified reports from Myanmar have periodically surfaced. It is regarded as critically endangered or extinct by the IUCN.

children.name: Salvadorina
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Salvadori's Duck, a cryptically patterned torrent duck endemic to the swift mountain streams of New Guinea, considered a relict waterfowl with uncertain family placement.

children.name: Salvadorina waigiuensis
children.description: Salvadorina waigiuensis (Salvadori's Teal) is a salvadorina species found in Asia region.

children.name: Sarkidiornis
children.description: A genus of 2 comb ducks found in tropical wetlands of Africa, South Asia, and South America, recognised by the large black knob on top of the male's bill that enlarges during the breeding season.

children.name: Sarkidiornis melanotos
children.description: Sarkidiornis melanotos (Comb Duck) is a sarkidiornis species found in Africa and Asia and South America regions.

children.name: Sarkidiornis sylvicola
children.description: Sarkidiornis sylvicola (American Comb Duck) is a sarkidiornis species found in South America region.

children.name: Sibirionetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Baikal Teal, a striking East Asian dabbling duck with an ornate green, yellow, and black face pattern in the male, breeding in Siberian boreal forests and migrating to East Asia.

children.name: Sibirionetta formosa
children.description: Sibirionetta formosa (Baikal Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Asia region.

children.name: Somateria
children.description: A genus of 3 large eider sea ducks of Arctic and subarctic coasts — Common, Spectacled, and King Eider — famous for the female's down used to line nests, which is harvested commercially as eiderdown.

children.name: Somateria fischeri
children.description: Somateria fischeri (Spectacled Eider) is a eider species found in North America and Asia regions.

children.name: Somateria mollissima
children.description: Somateria mollissima (Common Eider) is a eider species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Somateria spectabilis
children.description: Somateria spectabilis (King Eider) is a eider species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Spatula
children.description: A genus of 10 shovelers and teals with a near-cosmopolitan distribution, characterised by broad, spatula-shaped bills with dense lamellae that enable efficient filter-feeding on plankton and invertebrates in shallow water.

children.name: Spatula clypeata
children.description: Spatula clypeata (Northern Shoveler) is a dabbling duck species found in North America and Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Spatula cyanoptera
children.description: Spatula cyanoptera (Cinnamon Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Spatula discors
children.description: Spatula discors (Blue-winged Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in North America region.

children.name: Spatula hottentota
children.description: Spatula hottentota (Hottentot Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Spatula platalea
children.description: Spatula platalea (Red Shoveler) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Spatula puna
children.description: Spatula puna (Puna Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Spatula querquedula
children.description: Spatula querquedula (Garganey) is a dabbling duck species found in Europe and Asia and Africa regions.

children.name: Spatula rhynchotis
children.description: Spatula rhynchotis (Australasian Shoveler) is a dabbling duck species found in Australia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Spatula smithii
children.description: Spatula smithii (Cape Shoveler) is a dabbling duck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Spatula versicolor
children.description: Spatula versicolor (Silver Teal) is a dabbling duck species found in South America region.

children.name: Speculanas
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Bronze-winged Duck, a dark, chocolate-brown dabbling duck with a green wing speculum found along fast-flowing Andean and Patagonian rivers and lakes.

children.name: Speculanas specularis
children.description: Speculanas specularis (Bronze-winged Duck) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Stictonetta
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Freckled Duck, a cryptically speckled Australian waterfowl considered one of the most primitive living ducks, occurring in shallow freshwater swamps and lakes of southern Australia.

children.name: Stictonetta naevosa
children.description: Stictonetta naevosa (Freckled Duck) is a stictonetta species found in Australia region.

children.name: Tachyeres
children.description: A genus of 4 steamer-ducks restricted to the coasts and cold lakes of southern South America and the Falkland Islands, three species of which are flightless; they use their wings as paddles to propel themselves across water.

children.name: Tachyeres brachypterus
children.description: Tachyeres brachypterus (Falkland Steamer Duck) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Tachyeres leucocephalus
children.description: Tachyeres leucocephalus (White-headed Steamer Duck) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Tachyeres patachonicus
children.description: Tachyeres patachonicus (Flying Steamer Duck) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Tachyeres pteneres
children.description: Tachyeres pteneres (Fuegian Steamer Duck) is a teal/other species found in South America region.

children.name: Tadorna
children.description: A genus of 6 shelducks — large, goose-like ducks with striking patterned plumage — found from Western Europe to East Asia and Australia, often nesting in burrows and grazing on invertebrates along coasts and estuaries.

children.name: Tadorna cana
children.description: Tadorna cana (South African Shelduck) is a shelduck species found in Africa region.

children.name: Tadorna cristata
children.description: One of the most enigmatic and possibly extinct waterfowl in the world — a large, boldly patterned shelduck last confirmed alive in 1971 near Vladivostok, Russia, and now the subject of persistent but unverified sighting reports in the Korean Peninsula, northeastern China, and Japan. The male has a black-and-white body with a distinctive green-glossed crest; the female is similarly crested and warm brown. Known primarily from a handful of museum specimens collected in the late 19th and early 20th centuries from Japan and Korea. The cause of its apparent disappearance is unknown, though habitat destruction around Yellow Sea mudflats and historical hunting pressure are suspected. IUCN Critically Endangered (possibly extinct). Occasional unconfirmed reports from the Korean Demilitarized Zone keep hope alive that a remnant population may survive.

children.name: Tadorna ferruginea
children.description: Tadorna ferruginea (Ruddy Shelduck) is a shelduck species found in Europe and Asia and Africa regions.

children.name: Tadorna tadorna
children.description: The common shelduck is a waterfowl species of the shelduck genus Tadorna. It is widespread and common in the Palearctic, in Europe and northwest Africa mainly breeding on temperate coasts, saline pools and estuaries, and in Asia on inland salt lakes; increasingly it is also using open freshwater habitats inland. Wintering is in temperate to subtropical regions.

children.name: Tadorna tadornoides
children.description: Tadorna tadornoides (Australian Shelduck) is a shelduck species found in Australia region.

children.name: Tadorna variegata
children.description: Tadorna variegata (Paradise Shelduck) is a shelduck species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Thalassornis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the White-backed Duck, a small diving duck of sub-Saharan African and Madagascar freshwater lakes with dense aquatic vegetation, anatomically intermediate between stiff-tailed ducks and whistling-ducks.

children.name: Thalassornis leuconotus
children.description: Thalassornis leuconotus (White-backed Duck) is a thalassornis species found in Africa region.
